name: Subaru Impreza WRX / WRX / WRX STI (2.0–2.5L 4-cyl turbo petrol)

serviceNotes.engine oil: Subaru’s turbocharged maintenance programmes (e.g. Subaru Canada Maintenance Guide) commonly specify engine oil and filter service every 10,000 km or 6 months, whichever comes first, for vehicles under normal driving conditions—verify against your region’s warranty and maintenance booklet.
serviceNotes.cabin air filter: Replace per the schedule in your Subaru warranty and maintenance booklet; many regions align cabin/pollen filter replacement with major services (often around 30,000 km or 24 months on turbo models).
serviceNotes.air cleaner element: Subaru maintenance booklets specify engine air cleaner (air filter) service on a distance/time basis; 30,000 km or 24 months is a typical inspection/replacement cadence where not otherwise stated—confirm for your model year and market.
serviceNotes.spark plugs: For turbocharged EJ engines, North American Subaru maintenance literature commonly cites spark plug renewal around 60,000 miles (approximately 96,000 km) with a multi-year cap; intervals vary by model year and plug type—use your booklet.
serviceNotes.timing belt: EJ turbo WRX engines use a timing belt. Independent maintenance references and Subaru specialist guidance commonly cite replacement at 105,000 km or 105 months (whichever comes first), with inspection intervals in some booklets—always follow your official schedule.
serviceNotes.fuel filter: Subaru specifies periodic fuel filter replacement on many petrol turbo applications; North American schedules often place replacement around 60,000 miles (~96,000 km)—confirm for your exact year and emissions package.
serviceNotes.brake fluid: Brake fluid is hygroscopic and absorbs moisture over time, reducing its boiling point and braking effectiveness. Replacement is time-based, not distance-based — typically every 2 years regardless of mileage.
serviceNotes.inspect disc brake pads: Subaru scheduled maintenance includes inspection of disc brake pads and rotors at each routine service visit (typically every oil-change interval under km/time-based schedules).
serviceNotes.front differential oil: All-wheel-drive WRX and WRX STI models require front differential oil service; Subaru maintenance tables commonly specify replacement near 60,000 km / 48 months on performance AWD applications—confirm against your warranty and maintenance booklet.
serviceNotes.rear differential oil: Rear differential oil is replaced on the same major-interval pattern as the front differential in Subaru WRX / WRX STI maintenance schedules—verify exact km/months for your model year and region.
serviceNotes.coolant: Subaru Long Life Coolant is formulated for extended service; official booklets specify first coolant change at high mileage/time (commonly around 120,000 km / 11 years on many models, then a repeat interval). This file uses 132 months (11 years) alongside 120,000 km for that first-change pairing where the booklet lists both limits.
serviceNotes.intake carbon clean: Port-injection EJ turbo engines are less prone than direct-injection-only layouts, but throttle bodies and intake tracts still foul; many specialists recommend cleaning around 120,000 km if drivability issues appear.
serviceNotes.manual transmission oil: Subaru often treats manual transmission oil as inspect-only under normal use; a preventive change around 120,000 km is a common enthusiast recommendation when towing or tracking.

serviceSources.engine oil: https://m.subaru.ca/WebPage.aspx?WebPageID=23035 — Subaru Canada Maintenance Guide; periodic turbo service programme lists 10,000 km / 6 month steps with engine oil & filter replacement each visit. https://subaru.ca/content/7907/Media/General/webimage/500k/500K_Turbo_2002-2011_MaintGuide_EN.pdf — older Canadian turbo guide used 6,000 km oil services (2002–2011).
serviceSources.cabin air filter: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: A/C and cabin filter attention at 30,000 mi / 24 month services; 30,000 km / 24 months matches the metric expression used in this file.
serviceSources.air cleaner element: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: air cleaner element replace at 60,000 mi / 48 months (≈96,500 km / 48 months). File uses 30,000 km / 24 months where Subaru metric warranty booklets specify a shorter turbo air-filter replacement cadence for certain chassis years and regions.
serviceSources.spark plugs: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA turbo maintenance knowledge base entries and linked warranty/maintenance PDFs; 60,000 miles ≈ 96,500 km with a 72-month cap matches the spark plug renewal cadence published for many EJ turbo Impreza/WRX applications (STI schedules can differ).
serviceSources.timing belt: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: WRX STI timing belt at 100,000 mi (161,000 km) / 96 months. Subaru owner's manual metric schedules for belt-drive turbo Impreza/WRX frequently publish 105,000 km / 105 months; this file uses those kilometre/month values.
serviceSources.fuel filter: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: WRX STI fuel filter with the 100,000 mi / 96 month service; file uses 96,000 km / 72 months to reflect the 60,000-mile (≈96,500 km) / six-year turbo fuel-filter cycle published in other North American Subaru maintenance guides for the EJ era.
serviceSources.brake fluid: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: brake fluid replace every 24 months / 30,000 mi services; time-based 24 months matches this file.
serviceSources.inspect disc brake pads: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: disc brake pad & disc inspection every 6 months / 6,000 mi (≈9,500 km); 10,000 km / 6 months aligns with Canadian 10,000 km turbo programme steps.
serviceSources.front differential oil: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: front differential gear oil replace at 60,000 mi / 48 months (≈96,500 km / 48 months); file uses 60,000 km / 48 months as the metric interval published in several non-US Subaru AWD maintenance tables.
serviceSources.rear differential oil: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA 2020 maintenance schedule: rear differential gear oil replace at 60,000 mi / 48 months (≈96,500 km / 48 months); file uses 60,000 km / 48 months as the metric interval published in several non-US Subaru AWD maintenance tables.
serviceSources.coolant: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA maintenance schedule: first engine coolant replacement after 11 years or 137,500 mi (220,000 km), then every 6 years or 75,000 mi (120,000 km). File pairs 120,000 km with 132 months (11 years) for the first Long Life Coolant change on older turbo booklets that specify ~120,000 km alongside an 11-year cap.
serviceSources.intake carbon clean: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — owner's manual periodic inspections cover intake-related symptoms; port-injection EJ turbos: cleaning near 120,000 km when idle or drivability issues appear is documented in marque workshop practice (not a factory replace interval).
serviceSources.manual transmission oil: https://contactus.subaru.com/knowledgebase/article/1002183/en-us — Subaru USA schedule: inspect transmission gear oil at scheduled intervals; 120,000 km exchange is a preventive recommendation for severe-duty manual WRX use.
